Tests if dividing negative infinity by negative infinity results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion test that checks the result of dividing `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY` by itself using JavaScript's `isNaN` function. The test confirms that the result of this division is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is expected behavior in JavaScript when dividing infinity by infinity. The assertion uses the `assert` function to ensure that the expression evaluates to `true`, indicating that the division correctly results in `NaN`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on validating a specific mathematical operation involving special numeric values in JavaScript.
